tasks.title: Implement bytecode disassembler for call instruction extraction
tasks.description: Create module to disassemble BEAM bytecode using :beam_disasm and extract all MFA references from call-related opcodes
tasks.details:
Create BeamDcd.Disassembler module that uses :beam_disasm.file/1 to scan instruction streams.

Implementation:
```elixir
defmodule BeamDcd.Disassembler do
  @type mfa :: {module(), atom(), arity()}
  
  @call_opcodes [
    :call_ext, :call_ext_only, :call_ext_last,
    :bif0, :bif1, :bif2, :gc_bif,
    :make_fun2, :make_fun3
  ]
  
  @spec extract_references(Path.t()) :: {:ok, [mfa()]} | {:error, term()}
  def extract_references(beam_file) do
    case :beam_disasm.file(to_charlist(beam_file)) do
      {:beam_file, _module, _exports, _attrs, functions, _labels} ->
        references = 
          functions
          |> Enum.flat_map(&scan_function_instructions/1)
          |> Enum.uniq()
        {:ok, references}
      {:error, _} = error -> error
    end
  end
  
  defp scan_function_instructions({_function, _arity, _entry, instructions}) do
    instructions
    |> Enum.flat_map(&extract_mfa_from_instruction/1)
  end
  
  defp extract_mfa_from_instruction(instruction) do
    case instruction do
      {:call_ext, _arity, {:extfunc, mod, fun, ar}} -> [{mod, fun, ar}]
      {:call_ext_only, _arity, {:extfunc, mod, fun, ar}} -> [{mod, fun, ar}]
      {:call_ext_last, _arity, {:extfunc, mod, fun, ar}, _dealloc} -> [{mod, fun, ar}]
      {:make_fun2, {mod, fun, ar}, _idx, _old_uniq, _num_free} -> [{mod, fun, ar}]
      {:make_fun3, {mod, fun, ar}, _idx, _old_uniq, _num_free} -> [{mod, fun, ar}]
      {:bif, _name, _fail, _args, {:extfunc, mod, fun, ar}} -> [{mod, fun, ar}]
      _ -> []
    end
  end
end
```

Handle local calls by resolving against module's own exports when needed.

tasks.title: Implement abstract code analyzer for dynamic call detection
tasks.description: Create optional best-effort analyzer that walks Erlang abstract forms from debug_info to find additional references including literal dynamic calls
tasks.details:
Create BeamDcd.AbstractAnalyzer module for optional enhanced analysis.

Implementation:
```elixir
defmodule BeamDcd.AbstractAnalyzer do
  @type mfa :: {module(), atom(), arity()}
  
  @spec extract_references(Path.t()) :: {:ok, [mfa()], [String.t()]} | {:error, :no_debug_info}
  def extract_references(beam_file) do
    case :beam_lib.chunks(beam_file, [:abstract_code]) do
      {:ok, {_module, [{:abstract_code, {:raw_abstract_v1, forms}}]}} ->
        {mfas, warnings} = walk_abstract_forms(forms)
        {:ok, Enum.uniq(mfas), warnings}
      {:ok, {_module, [{:abstract_code, :no_abstract_code}]}} ->
        {:error, :no_debug_info}
      {:error, _} ->
        {:error, :no_debug_info}
    end
  end
  
  defp walk_abstract_forms(forms) do
    warnings = []
    mfas = 
      forms
      |> Enum.flat_map(&extract_from_form/1)
    
    # Detect dynamic dispatch patterns
    has_apply = Enum.any?(mfas, fn {m, f, _a} -> m == :erlang and f == :apply end)
    warnings = if has_apply, do: ["Module uses :erlang.apply/3 - may have false positives" | warnings], else: warnings
    
    {mfas, warnings}
  end
  
  defp extract_from_form({:function, _line, _name, _arity, clauses}) do
    Enum.flat_map(clauses, &extract_from_clause/1)
  end
  defp extract_from_form(_), do: []
  
  defp extract_from_clause({:clause, _line, _patterns, _guards, body}) do
    Enum.flat_map(body, &extract_from_expr/1)
  end
  
  defp extract_from_expr({:call, _line, {:remote, _line2, {:atom, _l3, mod}, {:atom, _l4, fun}}, args}) do
    [{mod, fun, length(args)}]
  end
  defp extract_from_expr({:call, _line, _fun, args}) do
    # Recursively walk args
    Enum.flat_map(args, &extract_from_expr/1)
  end
  defp extract_from_expr(tuple) when is_tuple(tuple) do
    tuple |> Tuple.to_list() |> Enum.flat_map(&extract_from_expr/1)
  end
  defp extract_from_expr(list) when is_list(list) do
    Enum.flat_map(list, &extract_from_expr/1)
  end
  defp extract_from_expr(_), do: []
end
```

tasks.title: Implement entrypoint and callback detection system
tasks.description: Create module to identify and filter compiler-generated functions, OTP behaviour callbacks, and framework-specific entrypoints
tasks.details:
Create BeamDcd.EntrypointDetector with automatic filtering and behaviour callback detection.

Implementation:
```elixir
defmodule BeamDcd.EntrypointDetector do
  @type mfa :: {module(), atom(), arity()}
  
  @compiler_generated [
    {:module_info, 0}, {:module_info, 1},
    {:__info__, 1}, {:behaviour_info, 1},
    {:__impl__, 1}, {:__struct__, 0}, {:__struct__, 1},
    {:__changeset__, 0}
  ]
  
  @otp_behaviours %{
    :gen_server => [{:init, 1}, {:handle_call, 3}, {:handle_cast, 2}, {:handle_info, 2}, {:terminate, 2}, {:code_change, 3}],
    :gen_statem => [{:init, 1}, {:callback_mode, 0}, {:terminate, 3}, {:code_change, 4}],
    :supervisor => [{:init, 1}],
    :application => [{:start, 2}, {:stop, 1}],
    Application => [{:start, 2}, {:stop, 1}],
    GenServer => [{:init, 1}, {:handle_call, 3}, {:handle_cast, 2}, {:handle_info, 2}, {:terminate, 2}, {:code_change, 3}],
    Supervisor => [{:init, 1}],
    # Phoenix behaviours
    Phoenix.Controller => [],
    Phoenix.LiveView => [{:mount, 3}, {:render, 1}, {:handle_event, 3}, {:handle_params, 3}, {:handle_info, 2}, {:handle_async, 3}],
    Plug => [{:init, 1}, {:call, 2}]
  }
  
  @spec is_compiler_generated?(mfa()) :: boolean()
  def is_compiler_generated?({_module, function, arity}) do
    {function, arity} in @compiler_generated or
    String.starts_with?(to_string(function), "MACRO-")
  end
  
  @spec detect_behaviours(Path.t()) :: {:ok, [module()]} | {:error, term()}
  def detect_behaviours(beam_file) do
    case :beam_lib.chunks(beam_file, [:attributes]) do
      {:ok, {_module, [{:attributes, attrs}]}} ->
        behaviours = 
          attrs
          |> Keyword.get_values(:behaviour)
          |> List.flatten()
        {:ok, behaviours}
      {:error, _} = error -> error
    end
  end
  
  @spec get_behaviour_callbacks([module()]) :: [mfa()]
  def get_behaviour_callbacks(behaviours) do
    behaviours
    |> Enum.flat_map(fn behaviour ->
      case Map.get(@otp_behaviours, behaviour) do
        nil -> []
        callbacks -> callbacks
      end
    end)
  end
  
  @spec is_entrypoint?(mfa(), [module()], keyword()) :: boolean()
  def is_entrypoint?(mfa, behaviours, config \\ []) do
    extra_entrypoints = Keyword.get(config, :extra_entrypoints, [])
    
    is_compiler_generated?(mfa) or
    mfa in get_behaviour_callbacks(behaviours) or
    mfa in extra_entrypoints or
    is_on_load_function?(mfa)
  end
  
  defp is_on_load_function?({_mod, :on_load, 0}), do: true
  defp is_on_load_function?(_), do: false
end
```

tasks.title: Build configuration system with file and CLI support
tasks.description: Create configuration loader that supports .beam_unused.exs config files and command-line flag overrides
tasks.details:
Create BeamDcd.Config module with schema validation and defaults.

Implementation:
```elixir
defmodule BeamDcd.Config do
  @type t :: %__MODULE__{
    beam_paths: [String.t()],
    exclude_deps: boolean(),
    include_test: boolean(),
    ignore_modules: [Regex.t() | atom()],
    ignore_functions: [mfa()],
    extra_entrypoints: [mfa()],
    treat_test_as_caller: boolean(),
    output_format: :text | :json | :github | :sarif,
    severity: :definite | :probable
  }
  
  defstruct [
    beam_paths: ["_build/dev/lib/*/ebin"],
    exclude_deps: true,
    include_test: false,
    ignore_modules: [],
    ignore_functions: [],
    extra_entrypoints: [],
    treat_test_as_caller: true,
    output_format: :text,
    severity: :definite
  ]
  
  @spec load(Path.t() | nil) :: {:ok, t()} | {:error, term()}
  def load(config_path \\ nil) do
    config_file = config_path || ".beam_unused.exs"
    
    base_config = %__MODULE__{}
    
    if File.exists?(config_file) do
      case Code.eval_file(config_file) do
        {config_map, _bindings} when is_map(config_map) ->
          {:ok, merge(base_config, config_map)}
        {config_list, _bindings} when is_list(config_list) ->
          {:ok, merge(base_config, Map.new(config_list))}
        _ ->
          {:error, "Invalid config file format"}
      end
    else
      {:ok, base_config}
    end
  end
  
  @spec merge(t(), map()) :: t()
  defp merge(config, overrides) do
    Map.merge(config, overrides, fn _k, v1, v2 -> v2 || v1 end)
  end
  
  @spec apply_cli_flags(t(), keyword()) :: t()
  def apply_cli_flags(config, flags) do
    Enum.reduce(flags, config, fn {key, value}, acc ->
      case key do
        :format -> %{acc | output_format: value}
        :include_deps -> %{acc | exclude_deps: not value}
        :strict -> acc  # handled at CLI level
        :config -> acc  # already processed
        _ -> acc
      end
    end)
  end
end
```

tasks.title: Create core analysis engine with export/used/unused computation
tasks.description: Build main analyzer that orchestrates Phase 1 (exports), Phase 2 (references), and Phase 3 (unused calculation) with ETS-backed storage
tasks.details:
Create BeamDcd.Analyzer as the main analysis engine.

Implementation:
```elixir
defmodule BeamDcd.Analyzer do
  alias BeamDcd.{ChunkParser, ReferenceCollector, EntrypointDetector, Config}
  
  @type analysis_result :: %{
    unused_functions: [mfa_info()],
    summary: %{
      total_exports_analyzed: non_neg_integer(),
      total_unused: non_neg_integer(),
      modules_analyzed: non_neg_integer(),
      warnings: [String.t()]
    }
  }
  
  @type mfa_info :: %{
    module: atom(),
    function: atom(),
    arity: non_neg_integer(),
    source_file: String.t() | nil,
    source_line: non_neg_integer() | nil,
    references_in_tests: non_neg_integer(),
    references_in_prod: non_neg_integer()
  }
  
  @spec analyze(Config.t()) :: {:ok, analysis_result()} | {:error, term()}
  def analyze(config) do
    beam_files = discover_beam_files(config)
    
    # Phase 1: Collect all exports (parallel)
    exports_table = :ets.new(:exports, [:set, :public])
    source_map_table = :ets.new(:source_map, [:set, :public])
    
    beam_files
    |> Task.async_stream(&collect_exports(&1, exports_table, source_map_table), timeout: 30_000)
    |> Stream.run()
    
    # Phase 2: Collect all references (parallel)
    references_table = :ets.new(:references, [:bag, :public])
    warnings_table = :ets.new(:warnings, [:bag, :public])
    
    beam_files
    |> Task.async_stream(&collect_references(&1, references_table, warnings_table), timeout: 30_000)
    |> Stream.run()
    
    # Phase 3: Compute unused
    all_exports = :ets.tab2list(exports_table)
    all_references = :ets.tab2list(references_table) |> Enum.map(&elem(&1, 0)) |> MapSet.new()
    all_warnings = :ets.tab2list(warnings_table) |> Enum.map(&elem(&1, 0))
    
    unused = compute_unused(all_exports, all_references, config, beam_files)
    
    # Cleanup
    :ets.delete(exports_table)
    :ets.delete(references_table)
    :ets.delete(source_map_table)
    :ets.delete(warnings_table)
    
    result = %{
      unused_functions: unused,
      summary: %{
        total_exports_analyzed: length(all_exports),
        total_unused: length(unused),
        modules_analyzed: beam_files |> Enum.map(&extract_module/1) |> Enum.uniq() |> length(),
        warnings: Enum.uniq(all_warnings)
      }
    }
    
    {:ok, result}
  end
  
  defp discover_beam_files(config) do
    config.beam_paths
    |> Enum.flat_map(&Path.wildcard/1)
    |> Enum.filter(&String.ends_with?(&1, ".beam"))
    |> filter_by_config(config)
  end
  
  defp filter_by_config(files, config) do
    files
    |> then(&if config.exclude_deps, do: filter_deps(&1), else: &1)
    |> then(&if not config.include_test, do: filter_test(&1), else: &1)
  end
  
  defp compute_unused(exports, references, config, beam_files) do
    # Build behaviour map for entrypoint detection
    behaviour_map = build_behaviour_map(beam_files)
    
    exports
    |> Enum.reject(fn {mfa, _source} ->
      {module, _fun, _arity} = mfa
      mfa in references or
      EntrypointDetector.is_entrypoint?(mfa, Map.get(behaviour_map, module, []), config) or
      should_ignore?(mfa, config)
    end)
    |> Enum.map(fn {mfa, source_info} ->
      {module, function, arity} = mfa
      %{
        module: module,
        function: function,
        arity: arity,
        source_file: source_info[:file],
        source_line: source_info[:line],
        references_in_tests: 0,
        references_in_prod: 0
      }
    end)
  end
end
```

tasks.title: Implement output formatters for text, JSON, GitHub, and SARIF
tasks.description: Create formatter modules that render analysis results in multiple output formats with proper source file mapping
tasks.details:
Create BeamDcd.Formatters namespace with protocol-based formatting.

Implementation:
```elixir
defmodule BeamDcd.Formatter do
  @callback format(map()) :: String.t()
  
  def format(result, :text), do: BeamDcd.Formatters.Text.format(result)
  def format(result, :json), do: BeamDcd.Formatters.JSON.format(result)
  def format(result, :github), do: BeamDcd.Formatters.GitHub.format(result)
  def format(result, :sarif), do: BeamDcd.Formatters.SARIF.format(result)
end

defmodule BeamDcd.Formatters.Text do
  def format(%{unused_functions: unused, summary: summary}) do
    # Group by source file
    grouped = Enum.group_by(unused, & &1.source_file)
    
    output = ["== Unused Public Functions ==", ""]
    
    file_outputs = 
      grouped
      |> Enum.sort_by(fn {file, _} -> file || "" end)
      |> Enum.map(fn {file, functions} ->
        module_name = format_module_name(List.first(functions).module)
        header = "#{file || module_name} (#{module_name})"
        
        function_lines = 
          functions
          |> Enum.map(fn f ->
            "  ├── #{f.function}/#{f.arity}        (0 references)"
          end)
        
        [header | function_lines]
      end)
      |> List.flatten()
    
    summary_line = [
      "",
      "Found #{summary.total_unused} unused public functions across #{length(Map.keys(grouped))} modules."
    ]
    
    (output ++ file_outputs ++ summary_line)
    |> Enum.join("\n")
  end
  
  defp format_module_name(module) do
    module
    |> to_string()
    |> String.replace_prefix("Elixir.", "")
  end
end

defmodule BeamDcd.Formatters.JSON do
  def format(result) do
    Jason.encode!(result, pretty: true)
  end
end

defmodule BeamDcd.Formatters.GitHub do
  # GitHub Actions annotation format
  def format(%{unused_functions: unused}) do
    unused
    |> Enum.map(fn f ->
      file = f.source_file || "unknown"
      line = f.source_line || 1
      "::warning file=#{file},line=#{line}::Unused function #{f.function}/#{f.arity}"
    end)
    |> Enum.join("\n")
  end
end

defmodule BeamDcd.Formatters.SARIF do
  # SARIF 2.1.0 format for IDE integration
  def format(%{unused_functions: unused}) do
    sarif = %{
      version: "2.1.0",
      "$schema": "https://json.schemastore.org/sarif-2.1.0.json",
      runs: [
        %{
          tool: %{
            driver: %{
              name: "BEAM Dead Code Detector",
              version: "0.1.0",
              informationUri: "https://github.com/beam_dcd"
            }
          },
          results: Enum.map(unused, &to_sarif_result/1)
        }
      ]
    }
    Jason.encode!(sarif, pretty: true)
  end
  
  defp to_sarif_result(f) do
    %{
      ruleId: "unused-function",
      message: %{text: "Unused public function #{f.function}/#{f.arity}"},
      locations: [
        %{
          physicalLocation: %{
            artifactLocation: %{uri: f.source_file || "unknown"},
            region: %{startLine: f.source_line || 1}
          }
        }
      ]
    }
  end
end
```

tasks.title: Create Mix task interface with CLI argument parsing
tasks.description: Implement mix unused_functions task with flag parsing, project compilation verification, and exit code handling
tasks.details:
Create Mix.Tasks.UnusedFunctions module.

Implementation:
```elixir
defmodule Mix.Tasks.UnusedFunctions do
  use Mix.Task
  
  @shortdoc "Finds unused public functions in BEAM files"
  
  @moduledoc """
  Analyzes compiled BEAM files to detect unused public functions.
  
  ## Usage
  
      mix unused_functions                    # Run with defaults
      mix unused_functions --format json      # JSON output
      mix unused_functions --include-deps     # Include dependencies
      mix unused_functions --strict           # Exit 1 if unused found
      mix unused_functions --config path      # Custom config file
  
  ## Options
  
    * `--format` - Output format: text (default), json, github, sarif
    * `--include-deps` - Include dependencies in analysis
    * `--include-test` - Include test files in analysis
    * `--strict` - Exit with code 1 if unused functions found
    * `--config` - Path to config file (default: .beam_unused.exs)
  
  """
  
  @impl Mix.Task
  def run(args) do
    {opts, _argv, _errors} = OptionParser.parse(args,
      strict: [
        format: :string,
        include_deps: :boolean,
        include_test: :boolean,
        strict: :boolean,
        config: :string
      ]
    )
    
    # Ensure project is compiled
    Mix.Task.run("compile")
    
    # Load config
    {:ok, config} = BeamDcd.Config.load(opts[:config])
    config = BeamDcd.Config.apply_cli_flags(config, opts)
    
    # Run analysis
    case BeamDcd.Analyzer.analyze(config) do
      {:ok, result} ->
        output = BeamDcd.Formatter.format(result, config.output_format)
        IO.puts(output)
        
        # Handle strict mode
        if opts[:strict] and result.summary.total_unused > 0 do
          System.halt(1)
        end
        
      {:error, reason} ->
        Mix.shell().error("Analysis failed: #{inspect(reason)}")
        System.halt(1)
    end
  end
end
```

tasks.title: Create escript CLI for standalone BEAM file analysis
tasks.description: Build standalone command-line tool that can analyze BEAM files without Mix project context
tasks.details:
Create BeamDcd.CLI module and configure escript in mix.exs.

Implementation:
```elixir
defmodule BeamDcd.CLI do
  @moduledoc false
  
  def main(args) do
    {opts, argv, _errors} = OptionParser.parse(args,
      strict: [
        format: :string,
        config: :string,
        help: :boolean
      ],
      aliases: [
        h: :help,
        f: :format
      ]
    )
    
    if opts[:help] or argv == [] do
      print_help()
      System.halt(0)
    end
    
    # Load config
    {:ok, config} = BeamDcd.Config.load(opts[:config])
    
    # Override beam_paths with CLI arguments
    config = %{config | beam_paths: argv}
    
    # Apply format flag
    config = if opts[:format] do
      format_atom = String.to_existing_atom(opts[:format])
      %{config | output_format: format_atom}
    else
      config
    end
    
    # Run analysis
    case BeamDcd.Analyzer.analyze(config) do
      {:ok, result} ->
        output = BeamDcd.Formatter.format(result, config.output_format)
        IO.puts(output)
        
        if result.summary.total_unused > 0 do
          System.halt(1)
        else
          System.halt(0)
        end
        
      {:error, reason} ->
        IO.puts(:stderr, "Error: #{inspect(reason)}")
        System.halt(1)
    end
  end
  
  defp print_help do
    IO.puts("""
    BEAM Dead Code Detector - Find unused public functions
    
    Usage:
      beam_unused [options] <beam_paths...>
    
    Arguments:
      <beam_paths>    Paths to BEAM files or directories (supports globs)
    
    Options:
      --format, -f    Output format: text, json, github, sarif (default: text)
      --config        Path to config file (default: .beam_unused.exs)
      --help, -h      Show this help
    
    Examples:
      beam_unused /path/to/ebin
      beam_unused _build/dev/lib/*/ebin --format json
      beam_unused --config custom.exs /app/ebin
    """)
  end
end
```

```elixir
defmodule BeamDcd.IntegrationTest do
  use ExUnit.Case
  
  @fixtures_ebin "test/fixtures/ebin"
  
  test "detects unused public functions" do
    config = %BeamDcd.Config{beam_paths: [@fixtures_ebin]}
    {:ok, result} = BeamDcd.Analyzer.analyze(config)
    
    unused_mfas = Enum.map(result.unused_functions, fn f -> {f.module, f.function, f.arity} end)
    
    # Should be reported as unused
    assert {TestFixtures.UsedFunctions, :public_unused, 0} in unused_mfas
    assert {TestFixtures.GenServerImpl, :unused_public, 0} in unused_mfas
    
    # Should NOT be reported (used)
    refute {TestFixtures.UsedFunctions, :public_used, 0} in unused_mfas
    
    # Should NOT be reported (GenServer callbacks)
    refute {TestFixtures.GenServerImpl, :init, 1} in unused_mfas
    refute {TestFixtures.GenServerImpl, :handle_call, 3} in unused_mfas
  end
  
  test "detects cross-module references" do
    config = %BeamDcd.Config{beam_paths: [@fixtures_ebin]}
    {:ok, result} = BeamDcd.Analyzer.analyze(config)
    
    unused_mfas = Enum.map(result.unused_functions, fn f -> {f.module, f.function, f.arity} end)
    
    # Function called from another module should not be unused
    refute {TestFixtures.UsedFunctions, :public_used, 0} in unused_mfas
  end
  
  test "detects fun captures" do
    config = %BeamDcd.Config{beam_paths: [@fixtures_ebin]}
    {:ok, result} = BeamDcd.Analyzer.analyze(config)
    
    unused_mfas = Enum.map(result.unused_functions, fn f -> {f.module, f.function, f.arity} end)
    
    # Function captured as fun should not be unused
    refute {TestFixtures.UsedFunctions, :public_used, 0} in unused_mfas
  end
  
  test "warns about dynamic dispatch" do
    config = %BeamDcd.Config{beam_paths: [@fixtures_ebin]}
    {:ok, result} = BeamDcd.Analyzer.analyze(config)
    
    assert Enum.any?(result.summary.warnings, &String.contains?(&1, "apply"))
  end
end
```

tasks.title: Add source file mapping and compile info extraction
tasks.description: Implement module to extract source file paths and line numbers from BEAM compile chunk for accurate reporting
tasks.details:
Create BeamDcd.SourceMapper to extract source information from BEAM files.

Implementation:
```elixir
defmodule BeamDcd.SourceMapper do
  @type source_info :: %{
    file: Path.t() | nil,
    line: non_neg_integer() | nil,
    compile_options: keyword()
  }
  
  @spec get_source_info(Path.t()) :: {:ok, source_info()} | {:error, term()}
  def get_source_info(beam_file) do
    case :beam_lib.chunks(beam_file, [:compile_info]) do
      {:ok, {_module, [{:compile_info, compile_info}]}} ->
        source_file = 
          compile_info
          |> Keyword.get(:source)
          |> to_string_if_charlist()
          |> make_relative_if_possible()
        
        info = %{
          file: source_file,
          line: nil,  # Line info requires abstract code or debug_info
          compile_options: Keyword.get(compile_info, :options, [])
        }
        
        {:ok, info}
        
      {:error, _} = error ->
        error
    end
  end
  
  @spec get_function_line(Path.t(), atom(), non_neg_integer()) :: {:ok, non_neg_integer()} | {:error, term()}
  def get_function_line(beam_file, function, arity) do
    # Try to get line number from abstract code
    case :beam_lib.chunks(beam_file, [:abstract_code]) do
      {:ok, {_module, [{:abstract_code, {:raw_abstract_v1, forms}}]}} ->
        line = find_function_line_in_forms(forms, function, arity)
        {:ok, line}
        
      _ ->
        # Fall back to debug_info if available
        case :beam_lib.chunks(beam_file, [:debug_info]) do
          {:ok, {_module, [{:debug_info, {:debug_info_v1, _backend, metadata}}]}} ->
            line = extract_line_from_debug_info(metadata, function, arity)
            {:ok, line}
          _ ->
            {:error, :no_debug_info}
        end
    end
  end
  
  defp find_function_line_in_forms(forms, function, arity) do
    forms
    |> Enum.find_value(fn
      {:function, line, ^function, ^arity, _clauses} -> line
      _ -> nil
    end)
  end
  
  defp to_string_if_charlist(value) when is_list(value) do
    case :unicode.characters_to_binary(value) do
      {:error, _, _} -> nil
      binary -> binary
    end
  end
  defp to_string_if_charlist(value), do: value
  
  defp make_relative_if_possible(nil), do: nil
  defp make_relative_if_possible(path) do
    cwd = File.cwd!()
    case Path.relative_to(path, cwd) do
      ^path -> path  # Path is not relative to cwd
      relative -> relative
    end
  end
  
  defp extract_line_from_debug_info(_metadata, _function, _arity) do
    # OTP 25+ debug_info format handling
    nil
  end
end
```

Integrate into BeamDcd.Analyzer to populate source_file and source_line in results.
